purpose: general-k composition GF unifying k=2 and k=3; exact count law P_k(n); k=4 marginal consistency

claims.P_k_count_law: THEOREM (proved by inductive avoid-span intersect symplectic-perp count)
claims.general_k_composition_GF: THEOREM (Moebius inclusion-exclusion over F_2^k subspace lattice with character-sum G_L)
claims.k2_unification: THEOREM (general-k code with k=2 reproduces thm:joint-gf)
claims.k3_unification: THEOREM (general-k code with k=3 reproduces thm:triple-gf)
claims.k4_pair_marginal_consistency: THEOREM (pair-marginals of k=4 GF reproduce k=2 GF at n=4)
claims.k4_triple_marginal_consistency: THEOREM (triple-marginals of k=4 GF reproduce k=3 GF at n=4)
claims.P_k_enumeration_verification: EVIDENCE (direct count matches closed form for small n, k)

guards.L1_exact_arithmetic: fractions.Fraction end-to-end; JSON rationals stored as strings; counts stored as integers
guards.L2_J_twist: character sum uses (-1)^{Omega(c_i,c_j)} with standard symplectic form; per-symplectic-pair contraction avoids dual-space confusion
guards.L3_query_class_hygiene: structural k-secret composition result only; no unrestricted SQ / Feldman inference claim
guards.L4_comparison_distribution: not engaged (no comparison distribution is transformed in this counting theorem)

pre_register_interpretation.scope: exact composition GF for ordered isotropic independent k-tuples; multi-pair SQ-level implication remains OPEN
pre_register_interpretation.k4_verification: k=4 consistency is verified on pair- and triple-marginals only; full 16-variable polynomial is not expanded
pre_register_interpretation.hardness_implication: structural counting theorem; does not by itself imply SQ hardness for full learning tasks

k2_unification.statement: general-k GF with k=2 reproduces thm:joint-gf
k2_unification.label: THEOREM

k3_unification.statement: general-k GF with k=3 reproduces thm:triple-gf
k3_unification.label: THEOREM

k4_marginal_consistency.statement: k=4 GF pair- and triple-marginals reproduce k=2/k=3 GFs at n=4
k4_marginal_consistency.label: THEOREM (algebraic marginal consistency; no full k=4 enumeration)
